summary: Executed backend + frontend validation for redesigned preview flow. Backend TMDB flow APIs passed; web preview flow works end-to-end with intermittent genre-load latency observed.

minor.issue: Intermittent slow response in UI path (genre grid sometimes remains in loading state for several seconds before populating).

ui_bugs.issue: Occasional prolonged loading before genre cards render; first two runs timed out with default waits though API later returned data.

issues: Genre cards are gradient/text-only in Flutter (`genre_flow_screen.dart`) instead of poster-backed cards requested in redesign.
Route parameters (`platform`, `contentType`, `genre`) are accepted by `MovieListingScreen` but not applied to fetch/filter logic (listing depends on `filteredMoviesProvider` state).
Filter chips are visual-only in `movie_listing_screen.dart` (no interaction logic for Top Rated / All Time changes).
OTT cards use text glyphs/colors instead of original platform logos as requested.

action_items: Optimize `/api/tmdb/genres` response time (current implementation makes sequential per-genre discover calls).
Wire Flutter movie listing to route-driven platform/type/genre selections and make filters functional.
Update Flutter genre cards to poster-backed visuals and OTT cards to logo assets per redesign requirement.
critical_code_review_comments: `TmdbService` in Flutter uses TMDB `/search/movie` and maps `contentType` as movie-only; this diverges from movie+series redesign flow.
`MovieListingScreen` currently does not consume incoming navigation parameters to drive query/filter behavior, risking incorrect content shown after `/ott -> /platform-home -> /genres -> /movies`.
Web preview (`frontend/src/main.jsx`) meets requested nav simplification and collection actions; no blocking frontend integration failure reproduced in final run.
test_credentials: Reviewed `/app/memory/test_credentials.md`: Google + Guest flows documented, no seeded email/password credentials.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest regression added and passing against public preview URL. UI flow passes end-to-end with longer waits; genre screen can appear stuck in loading on shorter waits, indicating latency/flakiness rather than hard failure.
rca of the issue: Genre-load flakiness RCA: `/api/tmdb/genres` loops through each genre and performs awaited discover requests sequentially, increasing response time variability. In UI this appears as prolonged 'Loading genres...' and caused initial Playwright timeouts. Mitigation: parallelize per-genre fetches (bounded concurrency), cache results briefly, and add frontend timeout/retry UX.
